evals.prompt: Fetch the transcript and metadata for this YouTube video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ — save the JSON output to the workspace and print a summary of: video title, channel, duration, and first 200 characters of the transcript.
evals.expected_output: A JSON file with ok=true, containing transcript_text, metadata with title/channel/duration, and a printed summary showing the Rick Astley video details.
expectations: The script is invoked using `uv run` and the skill's fetch_youtube_transcript_metadata.py script
The output JSON has ok=true and status=succeeded
The metadata contains a non-empty title field
The metadata contains a non-empty channel field
The transcript_text field is a non-empty string with at least 100 characters
The JSON output is saved to a file in the session workspace

evals.prompt: I need the transcript of this YouTube Shorts video: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/9bZkp7q19f0 — just fetch it and show me the first 300 characters of the transcript text plus the video title.
evals.expected_output: The script correctly handles the /shorts/ URL format, returns a JSON with ok=true (or a clear failure class if the video is unavailable), and the agent prints the requested transcript excerpt and title.
expectations: The script is invoked using `uv run` and the skill's fetch_youtube_transcript_metadata.py script
The --url argument receives the full shorts URL
The result JSON contains a video_id field (11-character string)
If ok=true, the transcript_text field is non-empty
If ok=false, the failure_class field is set to one of the documented failure classes

evals.prompt: Try to fetch the YouTube transcript for this URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=INVALID_ID_XYZ — tell me what happened and what the failure_class is.
evals.expected_output: The script returns a JSON result with ok=false and failure_class=invalid_video_target (or request_blocked). The agent reads and reports the failure_class and error fields clearly.
expectations: The script is invoked using `uv run` and the skill's fetch_youtube_transcript_metadata.py script
The result JSON has a failure_class field set to a non-empty string
The agent reports the failure_class value and explains what it means
The agent does NOT crash or exit without reading the JSON result
